handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 00-preflight.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only preflight engineering foundation: package manifest, framework configuration, strict TypeScript, Tailwind styling, shadcn/ui-compatible primitives, minimal smoke shell, unit smoke test, and Playwright smoke test.

Fixed verification dependency issues by pinning the project to Node 24/npm 11, removing obsolete local Node v23.3.0, upgrading direct dependencies to current compatible releases, and regenerating package-lock.json.

Resolved Vitest/Vite audit findings with vitest@4.1.9 and vite@8.0.16; resolved jsdom/canvas deprecated chain with jsdom@29.1.1 and react-pdf@10.4.1/PDF.js updates; resolved Next internal PostCSS audit by overriding Next's postcss dependency to ^8.5.15.
Updated Tailwind/PostCSS config for Tailwind CSS 4 and updated Next/Playwright configuration so production build and E2E smoke tests run without HMR console noise.

The visible UI is intentionally a placeholder for install/build/browser smoke checks and states that product modules are unavailable; no metronome, recording, sheet, reference, settings, or v1 behavior was implemented.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because a git commit cannot contain its own final object hash without changing that hash.
changedAreas: package.json scripts and dependency declarations for Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind, shadcn-compatible primitives, lucide-react, Zustand, Zod, Dexie, Tone.js, wavesurfer.js, react-pdf/PDF.js, Vitest, and Playwright
Next.js app scaffold under src/app with a minimal preflight shell page
shadcn/ui-compatible utility and primitive components under src/components/ui and src/lib
Architecture boundary placeholders under src/domain, src/services, src/infrastructure, src/stores, src/hooks, src/test/helpers, and test-fixtures
Vitest smoke unit test under tests/unit
Playwright smoke e2e test under tests/e2e covering render, desktop/mobile resize, and console error capture
Tooling config files: tsconfig, next config, Tailwind/PostCSS, ESLint, Prettier, Vitest, Playwright, .gitignore, .npmrc, .nvmrc, and README setup notes
knownRisks: The project now requires Node >=24.0.0 and npm >=11.17.0 with engine-strict=true; Node 23 is intentionally unsupported because current Vitest/Vite/jsdom toolchain releases do not support that odd Node line.
Playwright reports this OS is not officially supported and uses its ubuntu24.04 fallback Chromium build; chromium install and E2E both completed successfully in this environment.
Next.js 16/Turbopack build required unsandboxed execution here because sandboxed execution blocked an internal port bind; the unsandboxed build completed successfully.
Pre-existing unrelated line-ending-only changes remain in Design Notes/tech_design.md and docs/v1/module-status.json; they were not modified for this feature.

Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.
Fix pass for review findings on the previously verified 01-app-shell-home module; status is returned to implementation_done for independent verification.
Added a Home Import Sheet entry that navigates to the Sheet Library route shell and explicitly states that import workflow is not enabled yet.
Clarified the Sheet Library shell copy so import intent lands on a clear empty route shell without claiming real import support.
Made AppShell diagnostics/devtools affordance recoverable: hiding devtools for the session leaves a visible restore entry, and the in-memory state also resets on reload.

Expanded E2E and unit coverage for Import Sheet and diagnostics hide/restore regressions.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 02-quick-metronome.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only the Quick Metronome v0 contract scope: tempo, meter, subdivision, accent, countdown, tap tempo, metronome playback, quick recording, replay, quick session/recording metadata, persistence, and a minimal latest quick recording outlet.
Metronome UI calls a BrowserMetronomeService boundary that uses Web Audio scheduling; the UI does not directly call Web Audio scheduler APIs.

Recording UI calls a BrowserRecordingService boundary that uses MediaRecorder; E2E injects a controlled synthetic audio stream through getUserMedia and verifies a non-empty quick recording is saved and replayable.

Code review fix pass: BrowserMetronomeService now emits scheduled tick trace evidence with audioTime, BPM, expected interval, subdivision, and accent status so E2E can measure actual scheduled spacing instead of relying on UI text.

Code review fix pass: BrowserRecordingService now decodes the captured artifact after stop and records duration, sample rate, peak, RMS, estimated frequency, and silence status; E2E independently decodes the saved data URL and verifies the expected 440Hz synthetic input is present without obvious truncation or silence.
QA fix pass: BPM text input now keeps a draft value while typing and commits/clamps only on blur, Enter, stepper, or tap tempo, so values like 60 can be entered normally.
QA fix pass: time signature, subdivision, accent, and countdown are disabled with explanatory text while the metronome is running or counting down; BPM remains live-adjustable.
QA fix pass: empty latest-recording and recordings outlets now show a clearly marked playable demo synthetic 440Hz WAV sample that is not persisted or labeled as a user recording.

Review fix pass on staged QA diff: E2E now decodes the demo WAV data URL with browser AudioContext and verifies duration, RMS, peak, and estimated 440Hz frequency; unit tests also validate the RIFF/WAVE PCM header.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: E2E now covers BPM blur commit and draft-then-stepper behavior.

Review fix pass on staged QA diff: BrowserMetronomeService now uses a Tone.js adapter boundary with Tone.Transport scheduled callbacks and Tone.Synth triggerAttackRelease instead of hand-written Web Audio oscillator/setInterval scheduling.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: the Tone adapter stop path clears the scheduled event, stops/cancels Tone.Transport, and disposes the Synth to avoid global transport pollution.
Review fix pass on staged QA diff: unit tests inject a fake Tone adapter to prove scheduled callbacks trigger the Tone instrument path and emit trace evidence with matching scheduled time.
Quick recordings are saved with type quick, linked to a Practice Session with sourceType quick, and have sheetId null.
The /recordings route remains a minimal latest-recording outlet only; full recording library/search/waveform behavior remains for 03-recordings-review.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because a git commit cannot contain its own final object hash without changing that hash.
changedAreas: src/lib/quick-metronome adds pure control helpers, session/recording metadata creation, local persistence, BrowserMetronomeService, BrowserRecordingService, and recording replay service boundaries
src/lib/quick-metronome/metronome-service.ts adds scheduler trace events for timing verification
src/lib/quick-metronome/recording-service.ts and src/lib/quick-metronome/types.ts add decoded artifact analysis metadata
src/lib/quick-metronome/demo-recording.ts adds a real playable synthetic WAV demo recording marked with origin demo
src/lib/quick-metronome/control.ts adds BPM draft commit normalization used by the UI and unit tests
src/lib/quick-metronome/metronome-service.ts now wraps Tone.Transport and Tone.Synth behind the BrowserMetronomeService adapter boundary
src/components/quick-metronome adds the Quick Metronome client experience and latest quick recording outlet
src/components/quick-metronome/quick-metronome-experience.tsx adds BPM draft editing and locks pre-run meter/countdown controls while running
src/components/quick-metronome/latest-quick-recording.tsx shows a clearly marked demo synthetic recording when no saved user recording exists
src/app/quick-metronome/page.tsx now renders the real Quick Metronome workflow
src/app/recordings/page.tsx exposes the minimal latest quick recording outlet for v0 continuity without implementing the full recordings module
src/components/home/home-dashboard.tsx updates Quick Metronome and quick recording copy now that the module is implemented
tests/unit/quick-metronome-control.test.ts and tests/unit/quick-metronome-session.test.ts cover tempo bounds, meter/subdivision/accent/countdown validation, tap tempo, timing, and quick metadata

tests/unit/quick-metronome-metronome-service.test.ts covers Tone transport start/stop/cancel/clear/dispose lifecycle, scheduled callback rescheduling on BPM change, instrument triggerAttackRelease, and trace/trigger time alignment
tests/unit/quick-metronome-recording-analysis.test.ts covers decoded synthetic input frequency/amplitude detection and silent artifact detection

tests/e2e/quick-metronome.spec.ts covers visible controls, BPM draft input behavior including blur, Enter, and draft plus stepper paths, running-state control lockout, tap tempo, measured scheduler trace timing at selected BPM and after BPM change, demo synthetic replay/outlet with browser AudioContext decode, decoded controlled synthetic recording contents, replay, persistence after reload, microphone denial, and recording/metronome independence
tests/e2e/app-shell-home.spec.ts and tests/unit/home-dashboard.test.ts update Home assertions for the implemented quick recording path
knownRisks: Recording format and replay availability depend on browser MediaRecorder and HTMLAudioElement support; unsupported browsers show an explicit recording error.

Quick recording audio is persisted as a data URL in localStorage for the v0 latest-recording outlet; this is suitable for short quick takes but not a long-term storage design for the full recordings module.
The demo synthetic recording is intentionally not persisted and is labeled as demo synthetic/not saved to avoid presenting it as a real user take.
Metronome timing is now self-tested through scheduled Web Audio trace intervals in Chromium; independent verification should still confirm the same tolerance on the target environment.
Playwright's configured webServer uses port 3101 and exited after the E2E run; no separate dev server was left running.

acceptanceCriteria: BPM draft input accepted typing 6 without premature clamp, then committed on Enter, blur, and stepper interactions.

Empty-state demo and synthetic recording outlets were clearly labeled as demo/not saved, replayable, and browser-decodable.
BrowserMetronomeService stayed behind the Tone adapter boundary, cleared/disposed Tone lifecycle state, and emitted matching scheduled trace and trigger times.
Original quick-metronome contract still passed for timing trace, recording artifact, permission denial, persistence, independence flows, and console/pageerror cleanliness.

handoffNotes: Assigned feature: 03-recordings-review.
Coding model used: GPT-5.5 High.

Implemented only the Recordings / Review v0 contract: unified recordings list, empty state, search, type filter, details, playback, waveform, error markers, delete, Practice Again, and bad/missing audio state.
Recordings UI calls recording history, artifact/waveform, playback, and continue-target services; UI does not directly call IndexedDB/Dexie, raw file APIs, audio element internals, or wavesurfer.js.
Playback/waveform uses wavesurfer.js through RecordingWaveformPlaybackAdapter; artifact validation and simplified peaks are behind loadRecordingArtifactDetails.

Review fix pass: loadRecordingArtifactDetails now always validates and decodes audioDataUrl before returning playback-ready details; trustedPeaks are used only for waveform rendering after the real artifact decodes.
Review fix pass: decoded duration is compared with metadata duration using tolerance and mismatch is surfaced in the details UI as a warning instead of silently accepting stale metadata.

Review fix pass: recordings E2E no longer monkey-patches HTMLMediaElement.play to swallow rejection; it records unhandled playback rejections, verifies none occur, browser-decodes quick and sheet WAV artifacts, and exercises sheet playback.

QA fix pass: Practice Again now remains context-only for quick recordings; saving a later quick take creates a new recording entry and a new session instead of overwriting the source recording artifact.
QA fix pass: quick recording metadata duration now uses decoded artifact duration when available, so newly saved takes align saved duration with the actual playable artifact.
QA fix pass: quick recording persistence defensively assigns a fresh recording id if a caller tries to save a new take with an existing recording id, preserving the original recording and artifact.
Review low-finding fix pass: Practice Again E2E now captures quick-alpha's seeded artifact and immutable metadata before recording again, then asserts exact equality after the later take is saved.

Review low-finding fix pass: Recordings detail action is now labeled Practice Again in UI, contract, and tests to clarify that it returns to practice with source context and must not overwrite the historical recording.

The history repository preserves the existing quick-metronome local data boundary and adds marker-aware delete semantics so quick, sheet, and error marker records can coexist in one persisted snapshot.

E2E seeds real decodable WAV artifacts for quick and sheet recordings, checks decoded waveform peaks for quick, trusted peaks for sheet only after artifact validation, playback event evidence, marker ordering, Practice Again routes, Practice Again followed by another quick recording, delete persistence/artifact inaccessibility, bad audio, trustedPeaks with missing/bad audio, and duration mismatch warning handling.
No recording creation, sheet import/viewer, automatic scoring, export, archive, tags, favorites, or v1 analysis behavior was implemented.
Implementation commit hash is reported in the final handoff response because a git commit cannot contain its own final object hash without changing that hash.
changedAreas: src/app/recordings/page.tsx now renders the real Recordings Review experience instead of the placeholder latest-recording outlet
src/components/recordings-review adds the client UI for empty state, unified list, search/type filtering, details, waveform, playback controls, marker list, delete confirmation, and Practice Again
src/components/recordings-review adds decoded-vs-metadata duration mismatch warning display in recording details

src/lib/recordings-review adds pure history/filter/format helpers, local history repository, audio artifact validation/peak derivation, duration tolerance handling, and wavesurfer playback adapter boundaries
src/lib/quick-metronome/persistence.ts and src/lib/quick-metronome/types.ts preserve marker data and sheet-compatible session metadata when quick recordings are saved
src/lib/quick-metronome/persistence.ts now prevents accidental overwrite when a continued take is saved with an existing recording id
src/lib/quick-metronome/session.ts now stores quick recording duration from decoded artifact analysis when available

tests/unit/recordings-review-history.test.ts covers filtering, sorting, formatting, Practice Again targets, marker ordering, trusted peaks after decode, trustedPeaks with missing/bad audio, derived peaks, missing audio errors, and duration mismatch warnings
tests/unit/recordings-review-repository.test.ts covers local history loading and delete persistence for metadata, artifact access, and linked error markers

tests/e2e/recordings-review.spec.ts covers empty state, seeded real WAV recordings, browser decode duration/energy evidence, search, quick/sheet filters, details, quick and sheet playback without swallowed rejection, waveform evidence, markers, Practice Again routes, using Practice Again from a seeded quick recording then recording again without overwriting the original, delete persistence, artifact removal, bad audio, trustedPeaks with missing/bad audio, and duration mismatch warning
tests/unit/quick-metronome-session.test.ts covers decoded-duration quick metadata and duplicate-id save protection for continued takes
tests/e2e/quick-metronome.spec.ts updates /recordings assertions for the implemented unified review page
knownRisks: The v0 repository continues using the existing localStorage-backed quick recording boundary from 02; large or long recordings remain a future storage concern for Local Data rather than this review UI.
wavesurfer.js is isolated behind an adapter and verified in Chromium E2E; broader browser/audio codec compatibility should be rechecked when additional recording formats are introduced.
Sheet recordings are supported from seeded/persisted metadata for review and continue routing, but sheet recording creation remains out of scope until Sheet Practice recording work exists.
Hydration is guarded so SSR does not read localStorage; users may briefly see the empty shell before local recordings hydrate.
